title: Concrete Calculator
shortDescription: Calculate concrete volume, waste allowance, and bag count for slabs, footings, or post holes.
metaTitle: Concrete Calculator | Volume, Waste and Bags
metaDescription: Calculate concrete volume in m3, add waste allowance, and estimate the number of bags or ready-mix needed for slabs, footings, and posts.
intro: Use this concrete calculator to estimate how much concrete you need for a slab, footing, or post hole. Enter the dimensions, add a waste percentage, and get the volume plus an estimated bag count.
overview: This calculator converts length, width, and thickness into concrete volume in cubic meters. It also adds a waste allowance and estimates the number of bags based on bag yield. It is useful for planning ready-mix orders or bagged concrete jobs.
howItWorks: Enter the length and width in meters.
Set the waste percentage to cover spillage and uneven surfaces.
Enter the yield of one bag in cubic meters.
Review the concrete volume, volume with waste, and bag count.
useCases: Estimating concrete for slabs and garage floors.
Planning footing concrete for fences and small structures.
Calculating materials for post holes and column bases.
Comparing bagged concrete with ready-mix orders.

examples.title: Slab estimate
examples.description: A simple example for a small slab or patio base.

examples.title: Footing estimate
examples.description: Useful for strip footings or foundation pads.

faq.question: How do I calculate concrete volume?
faq.answer: Multiply length by width by thickness, then convert thickness from centimeters to meters.

faq.question: Why should I add waste?
faq.answer: Waste covers spills, over-excavation, and small calculation errors.

faq.question: Can I use this for ready-mix orders?
faq.answer: Yes. The volume with waste helps you estimate how much ready-mix to order.

faq.question: How do I estimate bags?
faq.answer: Divide the volume with waste by the bag yield, then round up to the next whole bag.

faq.question: Is this calculator suitable for post holes?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as you enter the hole dimensions as a simple rectangular equivalent or adapt the values to your project.
